text.title: Screen Reader Test Script Templates Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Screen Reader Test Script Templates methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every script pins device + OS + SR + SR version (e.g. 'iPhone 15, iOS 17.4, VoiceOver default').

rationale.source: WebAIM SR survey + Microsoft NVDA notes

rule.rationale: SR behaviour changes across versions; unpinned versions produce false drift.

rule.statement: Every step pins the exact gesture or keystroke (e.g. 'right-flick' / 'VO+→' / 'Ctrl+Alt+→'). Vague 'navigate to' steps are forbidden.

rationale.source: Apple VoiceOver gestures + NVDA user guide

rule.rationale: Different gestures land in different elements; explicit gesture is the only reproducible step.

rule.statement: Every step pins the expected announcement string. Wildcards allowed only for dynamic content (e.g. timestamps).
rule.rationale: Without expected string, pass/fail is opinion.

rule.statement: Every step pins pass/fail criteria (exact match / contains / regex). Steps with no criterion are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Criteria turn announcements into checkable assertions.

rule.statement: Each script maps to at least one WCAG SC (typical: 1.3.1, 4.1.2, 4.1.3).
rule.rationale: SR findings without SC mapping cannot be aggregated into audit reports.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-screen-reader-test-script-templates.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
